keyRules: Maximum height depends on weight: ≤250 g → 30 m; 250 g–25 kg → 60 m; 25–150 kg → 120 m
Drones weighing more than 250 g (all uses) require AAC registration — cost: US$5; in-person registration at Albrook
Recreational drones weighing ≤250 g: Registration is NOT required, but all other rules apply
Minimum distance of 8 km from airports and airfields
A minimum distance of 30 m horizontally and 10 m vertically from people
Always keep the drone within direct line of sight (VLOS)
Night flights are PROHIBITED without a specific AAC exemption
Civil Liability Insurance is MANDATORY for all transactions
Mandatory Informed Consent Form regarding: private property, indigenous reserves, protected areas, and the Panama Canal watershed
Commercial use requires an RPA pilot license in addition to drone registration
importantNotes: 📏 ALTITUDES VARY BY WEIGHT (unique in Latin America): Panama’s RACP has three maximum altitude levels based on drone weight: drones ≤250 g are limited to ONLY 30 m, drones weighing 250 g–25 kg are limited to 60 m, and only drones weighing ≥25 kg can reach the standard 120 m. Check your drone’s weight before planning your flight altitude.

✈️ TOURISTS — 14-DAY RULE: If your stay in Panama is ≤14 calendar days, you can apply for special AAC authorization without permanently registering the drone. Stays >14 days: AAC registration is mandatory for drones >250 g. Recreational drones ≤250 g are exempt in both cases.

🚢 PANAMA CANAL — SPECIAL RESTRICTION: The Canal basin (including the locks, Gatún Lake, and adjacent areas) requires a mandatory Letter of Informed Consent from the Canal Authority prior to any flight. The AAC will NOT authorize operations over this area without this letter. This also applies to private property, indigenous reserves, and protected areas.

🌳 PROTECTED AREAS AND MIAMBIENTE: National parks such as Soberanía, Volcán Barú, Coiba (a UNESCO World Heritage Site), and Darién require a specific permit from the Ministry of the Environment (MiAmbiente). The islands of Bocas del Toro and Guna Yala also have restrictions—coordinate with the local community before operating.

🏢 IN-PERSON REGISTRATION REQUIRED: Unlike most countries, AAC registration must be done IN PERSON at the Albrook office (Building 805). Bring: identification, the physical drone, the completed form, and US$5. Check appointment availability and processing times before your trip.

primaryLinks.title: AAC — Civil Aviation Authority of Panama
primaryLinks.description: Official website of the Civil Aviation Authority of Panama. Information on drone registration, RACP regulations, and procedures for domestic and foreign operators

primaryLinks.title: RACP — Civil Aviation Regulations of Panama
primaryLinks.description: Access to the complete Panama Civil Aviation Regulations. Section on regulations applicable to unmanned aircraft (RPA)

primaryLinks.title: AAC Albrook Office (in-person registration required)
primaryLinks.description: Building 805, Albrook, first floor — Aviation Safety Directorate. Drone registration MUST be done in person: bring identification, the drone itself, and the completed form. Cost: US$5

primaryLinks.title: AAC — Press Releases and Current Regulations
primaryLinks.description: Official AAC announcements regarding regulatory updates, temporary restrictions, and changes to drone procedures

secondaryLinks.title: STRI/Smithsonian — AAC Research Guide
secondaryLinks.description: Detailed guide from the Smithsonian Tropical Research Institute on the AAC authorization process, including the Informed Consent Form for restricted areas

secondaryLinks.title: MiAmbiente — Protected Areas (National Parks)
secondaryLinks.description: Panama's Ministry of the Environment. Special permits for drones in protected areas: Soberanía National Park, Barú Volcano, Coiba, Darién, etc.

secondaryLinks.title: Panama Canal — Official Information
secondaryLinks.description: Panama Canal Authority. The Canal basin requires a mandatory Letter of Informed Consent—without it, the AAC will not grant authorization

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the AAC's official sources before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. Panama enforces the Civil Aviation Regulations (RACP), which include weight-dependent maximum altitudes and special restrictions over the Panama Canal basin.
